c23b_rationale: ANNEX XIII enumerates 10 battery-specific performance parameters (rated capacity in Ah, voltage with temp ranges, power in Watts, lifetime in cycles, internal resistance, c-rate, etc.). ESPR Annex III has no comparable parameter list. Battery-specific entity separation is directly justified.

Art 27(6) requires: name, registered trade name, postal address, electronic contact. Annex III(g) requires UOI (Unique Operator Identifier). Annex III(h) requires UFI. 5+ structured fields explicitly mandated — simple string insufficient.

PACT spec defines version as 'Number (integer, range 0..2^31-1)'. Integer type explicitly forced. ESPR does not specify version format — free-form text. PCF model's nonNegativeInteger directly reflects PACT requirement.

ANNEX XIII requires detailed chemical composition (critical raw materials above 0.1%, hazardous substances). This motivates ChemicalMaterialEntity but the regulation does not specify Entity schema — it defines WHAT to report, not HOW to structure the data.

Annex I(b) defines 12+ spare part parameters (availability period, delivery time, compatibility, repair instructions). This motivates SparePartEntity but parameters are a framework — actual data fields delegated to product-specific delegated acts (not yet adopted).

ESPR requires product identifier (Art 10(1)(a)), GTIN (Annex III(c)), commodity codes (Annex III(d)). These provide regulatory basis for some DPP PartTypeEntity fields. However, Industry Core's PartTypeInformationEntity serves a different purpose (supply chain name mapping) — structural difference driven primarily by purpose, not regulation.

ANNEX XIII 1(m) requires only 'period for which the commercial warranty for the calendar life applies' — a single period value. Battery Pass WarrantyEntity (period + conditions + criteria) exceeds regulatory requirement. Transmission Pass nonNegativeInteger (simple period) is closer to regulation. Reclassified to Non-regulatory (WG over-implementation).

ESPR Annex I does not mention 'power' as a data field. Art 5-8 reference Annex I parameters but none specify power. Electric Drive/Transmission delegated acts not adopted. Entity vs integer difference is 100% WG design based on domain knowledge (motors produce power conditionally; transmissions transmit at rated capacity).
Same pattern as power. ESPR does not mention 'torque' anywhere in product data requirements. TorqueEntity vs nonNegativeInteger is 100% WG design.

PACT ProductFootprint is a flat structure — no 'General' grouping exists. Catena-X PCF WG created GeneralEntity by grouping PACT top-level metadata fields per SAMM convention. Additionally, quality_core General serves a completely different domain (quality management). Reclassified to Naming Collision.
Same pattern as power (case 8) and torque (case 9). ESPR does not mention ratedSpeed as a data field. float vs nonNegativeInteger difference is WG design based on domain knowledge.
Same pattern as power (case 8). ESPR does not specify maximumPower. MaxPowerEntity vs nonNegativeInteger is WG design.
Same pattern as torque (case 9). ESPR does not specify maximumTorque. MaxTorqueEntity vs nonNegativeInteger is WG design.
